    Added reflection to aim 6d526858-8f15-42b1-865e-1c7de5ef3ed9

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Tightened meta/preparing-for-release.md into a real publishability checklist instead of a strategy memo. It now tracks what is
  already done versus what still blocks release across onboarding, .bowman clarity, startup, port robustness, repo metadata, and
  optional integrations.

  I also completed the next two release-doc gaps:

  - added subdev/README.md and pointed the root docs at it so experiments are clearly outside the main onboarding path
  - expanded the git/data guidance in README.md with an explicit Git Workflow section covering what in .bowman is user-authored and
    commit-worthy, what is generated and should stay ignored, and the exception that agent-instruction injection can modify repo-
    root files like CLAUDE.md, .gemini/GEMINI.md, and .cursorrules

  I marked these aims done in Aimparency and added reflections:

  - 9cf44a40-4aa1-44a9-83d6-665d3e525592
  - 410344ec-1245-4636-991c-ec3b27760b03
  - 6d526858-8f15-42b1-865e-1c7de5ef3ed9

  Verification was a docs/code consistency pass against the backend implementation; no code tests were needed because this was
  documentation-only. One concrete remaining release issue I confirmed is that there is currently no top-level LICENSE file, so the
  checklist still reflects a real publishability gap.

  Next highest-leverage release task is .bowman/aims/c4b7dd26-f3df-4ab8-838c-97d7ac7f2bc2.json: audit repository metadata and
  public-facing package manifests.
